keyPoints: Provides a dedicated Relational Databases platform engineered to a multi-model graph database engine built on Spanner to run highly integrated network queries at global scale..
Integrates Spanner Graph capabilities directly into multi-tier cloud architectures, serving as a key component of the Databases, Analytics, and Data Lakes portfolio.
Encourages standard interface structures (REST APIs, Client SDKs, or console controls) to facilitate rapid cloud resource provisioning.
Manages relational schemas, NoSQL documents, and column-family tables with built-in replication and backup options.
Supports decoupled storage and compute architectures to process petabytes of analytical data without performance interference.
Implements Point-in-Time Recovery (PITR) to restore transactional databases to any microsecond for disaster recovery.
Ensures transactional integrity and schema validation using standard SQL engines with PostgreSQL or MySQL compatibility.
Provides read replica pools to scale analytical queries without impacting primary transactional write availability.
Supports automated snapshots, point-in-time recovery, and multi-zone high availability failover groups.
Governs access to Spanner Graph using standard IAM roles (e.g. roles/spannergraph.admin and roles/spannergraph.viewer) for fine-grained permissions.
Supports audit logging to record administrative actions and verify API configuration modifications for compliance reports.
Secures operational payloads using encryption at rest (Google-managed or Customer-Managed Keys via KMS) and SSL/TLS in transit.
Offers regional and multi-regional configuration options, duplicating service state across multiple zones to sustain outage events.
Enforces API quotas and rate limits programmatically to prevent resource starvation and noisy-neighbor interference.
Provides service recovery tools, regular status logs, and high-availability SLAs matching enterprise uptime goals.
Billed based on resource metrics (e.g., operations count, active connections, or gigabytes stored) allowing cost optimization.
Integrates with Cloud Monitoring and Cloud Logging to enable real-time alerting, metrics tracking, and diagnostic dashboards.
Enables command line administration through the gcloud CLI tool, allowing pipeline automation and scripting.

scenarios.scenario: An enterprise wants to construct a secure architectural pattern involving Spanner Graph that blocks public internet ingress and enforces strict access restrictions.
scenarios.solution: Deploy the Spanner Graph resource inside a VPC Service Control perimeter, routing connections privately using Serverless VPC connectors or internal gateways, and assign permissions using IAM roles.

scenarios.scenario: A development team needs to build automated CI/CD configurations that configure and audit Spanner Graph operations on a regular basis while tracking costs.
scenarios.solution: Use Cloud Build scripts executing 'gcloud' commands to provision the Spanner Graph resources, enable Cloud Audit Logs, and configure budget notifications at the project folder level.

commands.description: Create or configure a new Spanner Graph resource
commands.command:
gcloud spanner instances create prod-spannergraph-resource \
  --region=us-central1 \
  --description="Production Spanner Graph Resource"

commands.description: List active Spanner Graph instances and query configuration states
commands.command: gcloud spanner instances list --format="table(name, state, updateTime)"
